name: Steve Coleman
abstract: Steve Coleman (born September 20, 1956) is an American saxophonist, composer, bandleader and music theorist. In 2014, he was named a MacArthur Fellow.

concerts.label: Weill Recital Hall Centennial Festival: Rhythm In Mind

about_quotes.speaker: Abbey Lincoln
about_quotes.evidence: And I recorded it with Steve Coleman... At the Blue Note, we did 'Sophisticated Lady.'... and we recorded for Enja
about_quotes.doc_title: Abbey Lincoln — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Toots Thielemans
about_quotes.evidence: Greg Osby or Steve Coleman. The sound remains… their touch and their tone production is, without a doubt is from your side of the street.
about_quotes.doc_title: Toots Thielemans — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Lisa Parrott
about_quotes.evidence: the only thing I had lined up was some lessons with Steve Coleman, that’s who I had private lessons with.
about_quotes.doc_title: Lisa Parrott interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 22, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: I like him. He’s a fine young alto player. Yeah, I think he’s a comer. Plays real good.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
